title: Malpuwa (Sweet Pancakes)

ingredients.title: For Garnish
items: A drizzle of rabri (optional, for a royal touch)

instruction.title: Prepare the Batter
steps: In a bowl, combine flour, semolina, sugar, fennel seeds, cardamom powder, and salt.
Gradually add milk to form a smooth, slightly thick batter (like pancake batter).
Mix in grated coconut and chopped nuts if using.
Cover and let it rest for 30 minutes (helps semolina absorb moisture and gives a soft texture).

instruction.title: Make Sugar Syrup
steps: In a pan, combine sugar, water, and crushed cardamom.
Boil until it reaches one-string consistency.
Keep warm on very low heat.

instruction.title: Fry the Malpuas
steps: Heat ghee or oil in a wide pan on medium heat.
Pour a small ladle of batter into the pan to form small pancakes (slightly thick).
Fry until golden brown on both sides. Ensure medium heat to cook evenly.
Remove and drain excess oil on paper towels.

instruction.title: Soak in Syrup
steps: Immediately dip the fried malpuas into warm sugar syrup for 1–2 minutes.
Remove and place on a serving plate.

instruction.title: Garnish & Serve
steps: Garnish with chopped pistachios or almonds.
Serve warm with rabri for a festive touch.

chefTips: Resting the batter enhances flavor and softness.
Use medium-low heat while frying to prevent raw centers.
Malpuas can be made slightly thicker for a richer dessert or thinner for crispier edges.
Adding saffron to milk or syrup elevates the aroma.
